skillName: Position decimals on a number line (incl midpoint using a calculator)
misconceptionSummary: Students misread the scale interval and place decimals at integer tick positions.

steps.narration: The scale goes from zero to one with five gaps — each gap is worth zero point two. The student needed to place zero point four, but treated the second tick as if it said four. The scale is in tenths, not ones.
Divide the total range by the number of gaps to label every tick. Each step here adds zero point two — so the tick marks are zero, zero point two, zero point four, and so on.
Zero point four is two gaps from zero — that is the second tick mark. Now the position is correct because the scale was read before placing the point.

visuals.label: Read scale → label ticks → count gaps from zero

steps.narration: Zero point four sits at the second tick. Always calculate the interval value before reading off any position.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: reading tick numbers as 1, 2, 3, 4 when the scale is in tenths or hundredths. Calculate range ÷ gaps first — then label every tick correctly.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Tick marks do not automatically mean 1, 2, 3. Divide the range by the number of gaps to find what each tick is really worth.
